title: The Timezone Boundary Bug
short_description: A daily report is grouped on the UTC date instead of the business's local date boundary.
business_context: A retail team closes revenue by local business day, but the warehouse report is grouping on raw UTC event timestamps. Orders near midnight are landing on the wrong reporting date.
problem_statement: The source event timestamps are stored in UTC. The business day is defined by a store-specific UTC offset. Use the sample data to convert each event into the local business date and aggregate total revenue correctly.
student_task: Write a single read-only SQL query that returns daily revenue by `store_id` and local business date using the configured UTC offset.
learning_goals: Understand why UTC dates and local business dates are not interchangeable.
Apply timezone offsets before casting to the reporting date.
Handle midnight boundary rows safely in reporting logic.
production_logs: 2026-07-02 08:10:33 WARN store_id=10 revenue is lower than expected for 2026-07-01 local close
2026-07-02 08:10:33 WARN events around 00:00 UTC may be assigned to the wrong local business date
solution_query:
SELECT
  CAST(event_ts_utc + (utc_offset_hours * INTERVAL '1 hour') AS DATE) AS business_date,
  purchase_events.store_id,
  SUM(amount) AS total_amount
FROM purchase_events
JOIN store_timezones
  ON purchase_events.store_id = store_timezones.store_id
GROUP BY 1, 2
explanation: The local business date has to be derived after shifting the UTC timestamp into the business timezone. Casting the raw UTC timestamp directly to a date assigns late-night transactions to the wrong day and quietly distorts close-of-business reporting.
common_mistakes: Casting the UTC timestamp to a date before applying the timezone offset.
Applying the offset in the wrong direction and shifting events even farther away from the business day.
Aggregating by store only and losing the date boundary entirely.
hints: The fix is about the order of operations: shift first, cast second.
The timezone table tells you how many hours to move from UTC into local time.
Look closely at the two events just before 04:00 UTC.
